visualization.title: Students count by Department and Current Semester

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, s.current_semester AS current_semester, COUNT(s.id) AS student_count
FROM public.students AS s
JOIN public.departments AS d ON s.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name, s.current_semester
ORDER BY d.name ASC, s.current_semester ASC

issues: current_semester is encoded as nominal; if semesters have a natural numeric/order relationship, consider using ordinal (or quantitative) to preserve numeric ordering in legend/offsets.
The SQL uses an INNER JOIN so departments with zero students are omitted; if you need departments with zero counts included, use a LEFT JOIN and handle semesters accordingly.
With many semesters, grouped bars via xOffset can become crowded—consider stacked bars or faceting if visual clutter is an issue.

visualization.title: Students count by Department and Current Semester

result.sql:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, s.current_semester AS current_semester, COUNT(s.id) AS student_count
FROM public.students AS s
JOIN public.departments AS d ON s.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name, s.current_semester
ORDER BY d.name ASC, s.current_semester ASC

issues: current_semester is encoded as nominal; if semesters have a natural numeric/order relationship, consider using ordinal (or quantitative) to preserve numeric ordering in legend/offsets.
The SQL uses an INNER JOIN so departments with zero students are omitted; if you need departments with zero counts included, use a LEFT JOIN and handle semesters accordingly.
With many semesters, grouped bars via xOffset can become crowded—consider stacked bars or faceting if visual clutter is an issue.
